_meta.disclaimer: This matrix provides general guidance only and does not constitute legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney for specific compliance decisions.

MIT.note: API usage permitted under provider ToS. No source distribution issues.

Apache-2.0.note: API usage permitted under provider ToS.

GPL-2.0.note: API usage is not distribution — GPL does not apply to API calls.

GPL-3.0.note: API usage is not distribution — GPL-3.0 does not apply to API calls.

AGPL-3.0.note: AGPL's network use clause may apply if you embed model outputs in a networked service. Legal review recommended.

Proprietary.note: Subject to individual provider ToS agreements.

MIT.note: Apache 2.0 is MIT-compatible. Include NOTICE file if distributing.

Apache-2.0.note: Same license. No restrictions beyond attribution.

GPL-2.0.note: Apache 2.0 and GPL-2.0 are generally incompatible for combined works. Seek legal advice.

GPL-3.0.note: Apache 2.0 is compatible with GPL-3.0 when combined. Include NOTICE file.

AGPL-3.0.note: Apache 2.0 compatible with AGPL-3.0. AGPL network-use clause applies to the combined work.

Proprietary.note: Apache 2.0 permits proprietary use. Include LICENSE and NOTICE files.

Apache-2.0.note: MIT is permissive. Apache 2.0 patent grant applies to contributions.

AGPL-3.0.note: MIT is AGPL-compatible. AGPL network-use clause applies to combined work.

Proprietary.note: MIT permits proprietary use. Retain copyright notice.

MIT.note: Permitted for commercial use under 700M MAU threshold. Prohibited uses include certain harmful applications. Review Meta's Acceptable Use Policy.

Apache-2.0.note: Same as MIT — MAU threshold and AUP apply.

GPL-2.0.note: MAU threshold applies. GPL obligations apply to your code, not to Llama weights.

GPL-3.0.note: MAU threshold applies. GPL-3.0 obligations apply to your code.

AGPL-3.0.note: MAU threshold applies. AGPL network-use clause applies to combined work.

Proprietary.note: Commercial use permitted under MAU threshold. Above 700M MAU requires Meta license agreement.

MIT.note: MIT license. Standard permissive terms apply.

Apache-2.0.note: MIT-licensed weights. Apache 2.0 patent grant applies to your contributions.

MIT.note: Gemma Terms of Use restrict certain applications (harmful content, surveillance, etc.). Review Google's Prohibited Use Policy.

Apache-2.0.note: Gemma Terms apply on top of Apache-like grant. Prohibited uses include certain AI applications.

GPL-2.0.note: Gemma Terms impose additional restrictions that may conflict with GPL's requirement that no further restrictions be added.

GPL-3.0.note: Same as GPL-2.0 — additional use restrictions conflict with GPL's 'no further restrictions' clause.

Proprietary.note: Permitted for commercial use subject to Gemma Terms of Use. Review prohibited uses.

MIT.note: Non-commercial restriction conflicts with MIT's intent to permit all uses.

Apache-2.0.note: Non-commercial restriction incompatible with Apache 2.0 commercial use grant.

GPL-2.0.note: NC restriction incompatible with GPL's requirement for free redistribution.

Proprietary.note: Non-commercial use only. Internal/research use may qualify. Commercial deployment requires separate license.

MIT.note: Mistral Commercial License permits commercial use with attribution.

GPL-2.0.note: Check Mistral's terms for GPL compatibility. Additional restrictions may conflict.

AGPL-3.0.note: Commercial license terms may conflict with AGPL network-use requirements.

Proprietary.note: Commercial use explicitly permitted under Mistral Commercial License.
